248.reason: [When encountering a single '&', the code tries to read the next character without checking if idx is within bounds, which may cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException. Additionally, after detecting an error, it returns immediately but does not stop processing other lines; however, more critically, it fails to handle the case where '&' is at the end of the line (idx == line.length()), leading to an out-of-bounds access.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&', the code tries to read the next character without checking if idx is within bounds, which may cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException. Additionally, after detecting an error, it returns immediately but does not stop processing other lines; however, more critically, it fails to handle the case where '&' is at the end of the line (idx == line.length()), leading to an out-of-bounds access.]

261.reason: [Same issue as with '&': when '|' appears at the end of a line, line.charAt(idx++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because idx is already equal to line.length(). The code does not check whether idx < line.length() before accessing line.charAt(idx).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as with '&': when '|' appears at the end of a line, line.charAt(idx++) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException because idx is already equal to line.length(). The code does not check whether idx < line.length() before accessing line.charAt(idx).]
